programmes.show_description: 1953. Comedy. Director Noel Langley. Stars Joan Collins, George Cole, Kenneth More & Hattie Jacques. A British cast lead this story about a shipwrecked group on a tropical island.
1945. In this charming short video Charlie Chester promotes the importance of orange juice for children under five.
1963. Who Killed Julian Buck? Directed by Don Weis. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. One of America's most respected authors, is found strangled to death in his study.
1965. Two Of A Kind. Gig Young, David Niven and Charles Boyer are The Rogues, members of two related families of international forgers and conmen.
Betrothal. 1955. Betrothal. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Rufus Cruickshank. Once again Sir Richard of the Lea has financial troubles due to his heavy gambling.
The Articles of War. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Eric Pohlmann & Brian Rawlinson. The island is running out of food and a Spanish naval ship threatens to destroy the next shipment.
The Bear. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. When his son joins Tell's group and refuses to return home, the Bear, swears to seek revenge on Tell and his followers.
Cave-In. Director: Aaron Spelling. Clay Horne, an old friend of Ringo's rents an old codger's mine as a refuge against four men hired to avenge another man's death by Horne in a fair fight.
1946. Comedy. Director: Irving Pichel. Stars Charles Coburn, Joan Bennett & William Eythe. A retired army officer returns to his hometown, vowing to make change after seeing a lack of civic pride.
1959. Drama. Directed by Terry Bishop. Stars Keith Andes, Hazel Court, Michael Gough, Julia Arnall & Patricia Jessel. A Mayfair dress designer arranges for the theft of valuable jewellery.
1962. Drama. Director: Lance Comfort. Stars Liz Fraser, Kenneth Griffith, Peter Reynolds. When a scam goes wrong and someone gets murdered, a student gets accused of the homicide.
1959. Crime. Directed by Max Varnel. Stars Philip Friend & Susan Beaumont. When a teacher is wrongly accused of murdering a pupil, he must clear his name and save the school from another tragedy.
The Robbers of Ashdown Forest. 1962. Stars Dermot Walsh & Robin Hunter. King Richard hears news that Prince John has inspired another rebellion and recklessly journeys to meet the threat.
1943. War. Directed by Jeffery Dell. Stars Jane Baxter, Clifford Evans, Philip Friend. Based on a true story. A Belgian Pilot's daring attempt to retrieve the Colours from Nazi-occupied Belgium.
The Adolescent. 1954. Stars Ida Lupino, Robert Arthur and Hugh Sanders. A school disciplinarian and aunt to a troubled teenage pupil, must investigate her nephew's misconduct.
1945. Drama. Directed by Leslie Arliss. Stars Margaret Lockwood, Griffith Jones & James Mason. 17th Century beauty Barbara Worth leads a double life to relieve her boredom.
1959. Action. Director: Paul Wendkos. Stars Cliff Robertson, Gia Scala & Teru Shimada. In 1942, submarine commander Jeff Conway secretly photographs Japanese aircraft carriers in the Coral Sea.
Snapshot. 1955. Stars Ron Randell, Dorothy Gordon & Philip Friend. A hot day and an even hotter argument between Stella and Martin, after Martin suggests that they see each other less often...
Mike and Noel travel back to 1960 for a rare reel; Hughie Green's Happy Travellers Club. Enjoy a beautiful home movie of Venice from 1963 and we jet across the pond to see Paul Anka!

programmes.show_name: Francis Durbridge's A Game of Murder
programmes.show_description: 1966. Stars Gerald Harper, Conrad Phillips & David Burke. When a former athlete's mysterious death seems suspicious, his detective son investigates.
2023. Thriller. Director: Michael Wright. Stars Paul McGann, Tara Fitzgerald & Murray Melvin. An undertaker makes the wrong call at a moral crossroad and has to dispose of a gangster's victims.
1949. Western. Stars Randolph Scott, Bill Williams & Victor Jory. Jim Dancer, one of Quantrill's Raiders, stages attacks on Kansas on behalf of the fallen Confederacy.
Do Me A Favour. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Hazel's cousin Herbert arrives in London and Budgie is bribed into taking him on a guided tour.
1950. True story of a woman imprisoned in several Japanese war camps. She struggles to protect her young son. Stars Claudette Colbert.
1966. Comedy. Directed by Francis A Searle. Stars Barbara Mullen and Eric Woodburn. A spinster has been proclaimed dead - but is very much alive...
1957. Musical. A disc-jockey gets his own TV show and becomes a star. Starring Jackie Collins and featuring many musical acts of the 50's.
1965. Invitation To Limbo. Crime series produced by Aaron Spelling & stars Anne Francis. An electronics firm hires Honey to find an industrial spy who has been leaking company secrets.
Broomstick Wedding. 1956. Stars Scott Forbes & Mike Connors. An angry farmer mistakenly believes that Jim is the man who 'dishonoured' his daughter and has to right his wrong by marrying her.
1943. Romantic Comedy. Directed by George Stevens. Stars Jean Arthur, Joel McCrea & Charles Coburn. During the wartime housing shortage, a woman sublets half her apartment to two bachelors.
1964. Drama. Directed by Frank Nesbitt. Stars Dan Duryea, Isa Miranda, Gwen Watford & Peter Madden. In this great murder mystery, the only clue to the killer's identity is his shoes!
1949. Drama. Directed by George King. Stars Douglass Montgomery, Hazel Court & Patricia Burke. An ex-serviceman plans to escape his faithless wife.
1942. Drama. Director: Lawrence Huntington. Stars Clifford Evans, Patricia Roc, David Farrar, Robert Beatty, William Hartnell. Two bank robbers try to find their partner, who fled to London.
The Secret Death. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. When his wife is captured by Gessler's troops and sentenced to death, Tell offers himself in exchange.
1957. Drama. Director Roy Ward Baker. Stars Hardy Kruger, Colin Gordon & Michael Goodliffe. A German flight officer is shot down over England and makes numerous attempts to escape to fight again.
1956. Drama. Director: Pierre Gaspard-Huit. Stars Brigitte Bardot, Micheline Presle & Louis Jourdan. A country girl is discovered by a Paris magazine and becomes a top model.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life It's Odds on the Fish
programmes.show_description: 1968. A look into the art of angling, with a consideration for the problems of ensuring that angling continues as a form of sport and relaxation.
Dutchman's Reef. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Leonard Teale. Mrs. Culbert summons Cobb to search for her missing son but he is not interested until they come to an agreement.
The Terbuf Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo and Kuryakin are spending some time off in Rome when Solo meets an old flame who is in need of help.
The Nightlifers. Stars Derek Fowlds, John Gregson, Alexander Davion and Daphne Anderson. A group of rich young people go from endless parties aboard a houseboat to violent crimes in the city.
Murders in Collioure - Meurtres à Collioure. 2015. Director: Bruno Garcia. Stars Stéphane Freiss & Héléna Noguerra. A young woman is found murdered echoing a local medieval legend of a witch.
A cursed night is in store for all Cellar Dwellers, as Caroline Munro invites us into the world of female vampires, with The World of Hammer episode Vamp.
Vamp. 1994. Narrated by Oliver Reed. A look at Hammer's depiction of female vampires, which was consistently groundbreaking and controversial, with the fine line trodden between desire and evil.
1977. Director: Richard Loncraine. Stars Mia Farrow, Kier Dullea & Tom Conti. Mourning Julia Lofting moves to London to restart her life, only to be haunted by the ghosts of other children.
